task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: LastReEncStat uses the last_reenc_stat enumeration, where values 0-3 are Success, Read Error, Write Error, and Verify Error, and values 4-7 are reserved. GeneralStatus uses the gen_status enumeration; values 0-6, 32, 33, and 34 are named, while values 7-31 are reserved. Therefore a successful Locking.Get returning reserved LastReEncStat or GeneralStatus values is not protocol-compliant.

source_snippets.text: ... process. Table 86 gen_status | Column Value | Associated Value | Meaning | |--------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 0 | None | | | 1 | pending_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 2 | active_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 3 | active_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND PAUSE_req was detected | | 4 | pend_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a PAUSE_req value was detected | | 5 | pend_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last state was PENDING | | 6 | key_error | ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND valid keys were not found in any C_* table OR insufficient access control granted for reading C_* table. | | 7 to 31 | reserved | | | 32 | wait_AvailableKeys | keys are not available | | 33 | wait_for_TPer_resources| TPer_Ready condition is not True | | 34 | active_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE | | 34 to 63 | reserved | | The enumeration values are associated as defined in table Table 87. Values 0-31 are valid for the PAUSED state, value 32-63 are valid for the PENDING state (see 5.7.3.3). Table 87 gen_status Enumeration Values ```markdown | Column Value | Associated Value | Meaning | |--------------|--------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 0 | None | | | 1 | pending_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 2 | active_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 3 | active_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND PAUSE_req was detected | | 4 | pend_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a PAUSE_req value was detected | | 5 | pend_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last state was PENDING | | 6 | key_error | ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND valid keys were not found in any C_* table OR insufficient access control granted for reading C_* table. | | 7 to 31 | reserved | | | 32 | wait_AvailableKeys | keys are not available | | 33 | wait_for_TPer_resources | TPer_Ready condition is not True | | 34 | active_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE | | 34 to 63 | reserved | | ```

5.1.3.45 last_reenc_stat This enumeration identifies the last attempted re-encryption step. Table 100 last_reenc_stat ```markdown | UID | Name | Format | |--------------------------------|----------------|-------------------------| | 00 00 00 05 00 00 04 11 | last_reenc_stat| Enumeration_Type, 0, 7 | ``` The enumeration values are associated as defined in Table 101. Table 101 last_reenc_stat Enumeration Values | Enumeration Value | Associated Value | |-------------------|------------------| | 0 | Success | | 1 | Read Error | | 2 | Write Error | | 3 | Verify Error | | 4-7 | Reserved |

5.7.2.2.19 LastReEncStat This column value defines the last good re-encryption read-modify-write-verify sequence. This column value is only valid when the ReencryptState is COMPLETED, PENDING or PAUSED. When the LastReEncStat value is anything other than SUCCESS, the value of LastReEncryptLBA+1 is the LBA in error or the LBA located at RangeStart if LastReEncryptLBA contains 0xFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFF. Valid values are defined in table Table 101. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.7.2.2.20 GeneralStatus This field defines why the re-encryption operation arrived at the PAUSED or PENDING state. The value in this column is only valid when in the PAUSED or PENDING state. The values are defined in table Table 87. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity

Table 87 gen_status Enumeration Values ```markdown | Column Value | Associated Value | Meaning | |--------------|--------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 0 | None | | | 1 | pending_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 2 | active_tper_error | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND a TPer_Error_Detect condition was detected | | 3 | active_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE AND PAUSE_req was detected | | 4 | pend_pause_requested | Last ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND a PAUSE_req value was detected | | 5 | pend_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last state was PENDING | | 6 | key_error | ReEncryptState value was PENDING AND valid keys were not found in any C_* table OR insufficient access control granted for reading C_* table. | | 7 to 31 | reserved | | | 32 | wait_AvailableKeys | keys are not available | | 33 | wait_for_TPer_resources | TPer_Ready condition is not True | | 34 | active_reset_stop_detect | A reset condition AND its associated ContOnReset configuration does not allow re-encryption to continue AND last ReEncryptState value was ACTIVE | | 34 to 63 | reserved | | ```

5.1.3.45 last_reenc_stat This enumeration identifies the last attempted re-encryption step. Table 100 last_reenc_stat ```markdown | UID | Name | Format | |--------------------------------|----------------|-------------------------| | 00 00 00 05 00 00 04 11 | last_reenc_stat| Enumeration_Type, 0, 7 | ``` The enumeration values are associated as defined in Table 101. Table 101 last_reenc_stat Enumeration Values | Enumeration Value | Associated Value | |-------------------|------------------| | 0 | Success | | 1 | Read Error | | 2 | Write Error | | 3 | Verify Error | | 4-7 | Reserved |

5.7.2.2.19 LastReEncStat This column value defines the last good re-encryption read-modify-write-verify sequence. This column value is only valid when the ReencryptState is COMPLETED, PENDING or PAUSED. When the LastReEncStat value is anything other than SUCCESS, the value of LastReEncryptLBA+1 is the LBA in error or the LBA located at RangeStart if LastReEncryptLBA contains 0xFFFFFFFF_FFFFFFFF. Valid values are defined in table Table 101. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

5.7.2.2.20 GeneralStatus This field defines why the re-encryption operation arrived at the PAUSED or PENDING state. The value in this column is only valid when in the PAUSED or PENDING state. The values are defined in table Table 87. This column SHALL NOT be modifiable by the host.

task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: LastReEncStat uses the last_reenc_stat enumeration, where values 0-3 are Success, Read Error, Write Error, and Verify Error, and values 4-7 are reserved. GeneralStatus uses the gen_status enumeration; values 0-6, 32, 33, and 34 are named, while values 7-31 are reserved. Therefore a successful Locking.Get returning reserved LastReEncStat or GeneralStatus values is not protocol-compliant.
